### Post by vh1 on 2005-10-17
I'm eventually trying to get two monitors set up, but for now I'm to get them working one at a time

I've got an old HP Pavilion with an integrated Intel 82810 Graphics Card, and a GeForce 2 in a pci slot.

the nvidia card works fine, in fact I'm using it now. but when I try to use the intel card, my computer hard-freezes.

my xorg.conf is pretty standard:
```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following commands:
#
#   cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.custom
#   sudo sh -c 'md5sum /etc/X11/xorg.conf >/var/lib/xfree86/xorg.conf.md5sum'
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/CID"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
        # paths to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/CID"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"GLcore"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"dbe"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"GeForce"
	Driver		"nv"
	BusID		"PCI:1:14:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:1:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Digital"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"GeForce"
	Monitor		"Digital"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	Option	"Xinerama" "On"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```
that setup works fine

it's when I change the Device from "Geforce" to "Intel" in the Screen section and try to startx my computer just hangs

I tried doing an `lspci -vv`, and sure enough that told me that the intel card was disabled ("I/O- MEM-", memory disabled). so I did `setpci -d xxxx:xxxx command=3`, then did `lspci -vv` again and it told me that "I/O+ Mem+", and the "[disabled]" tag was removed from the memory.

I tried running startx again and still my computer hung there

I have no clue where to go from here. it works fine in windows xp

bump?

### Post by Leopard on 2005-10-27
Being a newbie to linux (Ubuntu 5.10 is my first install),  I was amazed at how difficult setting up a second monitor can be.  I ended up getting mine to work though!

Here's where my xorg.conf is different (note I have a dual output video card, which is why the BusID is the same for both devices- well, that's what I assume anyway):

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon X800 Pro (R420 JI)"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
**	Screen 0**
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon X800 Pro (R420 JI)_2"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
**	Screen 1**
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Mitsubishi"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Philips"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Mitsubishi"
	Device		"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon X800 Pro (R420 JI)"
	Monitor		"Mitsubishi"
	DefaultDepth	24
[*then all the stuff about depth & modes for that monitor*]

*******then I have a 2nd section for the 2nd monitor******
Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Philips"
	Device		"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon X800 Pro (R420 JI)_2"
	Monitor		"Philips"
	DefaultDepth	24
[*stuff as above*]

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Twin_Layout"
	**Screen		"Mitsubishi"**
	**Screen		"Philips" LeftOf "Mitsubishi"**
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection


You can probaly tell I have 2 different monitors, luckily both are LCD's that run native 1280x1024. so I just copied the first screen section to make the second one.  I also had to fool around a lot to get the monitors to be the correct orientation :p

---

### Post by vh1 on 2005-10-28
those steps helped a bit. X actually loads now, as opposed to just crashing.
still, no display on the second monitor though

here's my new xorg.conf:
```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following commands:
#
#   cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.custom
#   sudo sh -c 'md5sum /etc/X11/xorg.conf >/var/lib/xfree86/xorg.conf.md5sum'
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/CID"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
        # paths to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/CID"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"GLcore"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"dbe"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"GeForce"
	Driver		"nv"
	BusID		"PCI:1:11:0"
	Screen	0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:1:0"
	Screen 1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Digital"
	Option		"DPMS"
#	HorizSync	28-49
#	VertRefresh	43-72
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier "Compaq"
	Option "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Digital"
	Device		"GeForce"
	Monitor		"Digital"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		# Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
		Modes		"1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
#		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
		Modes		"1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
#		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
		Modes		"1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
#		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
		Modes		"1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Compaq"
	Device "Intel"
	Monitor "Compaq"
	DefaultDepth 24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth 24
		Modes "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Digital"
	Screen "Compaq" RightOf "Digital"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	Option	"Xinerama" "On"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```

X seems to pick up both cards fine at the start
(from /var/log/Xorg.0.log)
```
(--) PCI: (0:1:0) Intel Corp. 82810 CGC [Chipset Graphics Controller] rev 3, Mem @ 0xec000000/26, 0xe8000000/19
(--) PCI:*(1:11:0) nVidia Corporation NV11 [GeForce2 MX/MX 400] rev 178, Mem @ 0xe9000000/24, 0xf0000000/27
```

but then later on it says:
```
(WW) I810: No matching Device section for instance (BusID PCI:0:1:0) found
(--) Chipset i810 found
(EE) Screen 1 deleted because of no matching config section.
(II) UnloadModule: "i810"
```

and I'm stumped, yet again

---

### Post by vh1 on 2005-10-28
the problem is definitely that PCI card

commenting out the Screen lines in the device sections just causes X to go back to hanging, so I turned off the computer and removed the PCI video card and now the card that was being problematic before now works fine. but when I plug in the pci card again, voila, only it works

I would like to be able to say this is a hardware issue, but this is the second pair of video cards I've tried to run dual displays on and had fail in X but work fine in windows

---

### Post by saivert on 2005-12-06
I'm using nVidia GeForce 6600 Go with "nvidia" driver obtained by typing
  sudo apt-get install nvidia-glx nvidia-settings
in a terminal window.

Question #1:
I have activated "TwinView" option inside the Device section for my card. But I see people put the very same Option line inside their Screen sections as well? What is the difference?

Question #2:
What's the difference between adding to X screens and using nVidia TwinView?
